title: MP Eva statue at the end of Death
display_title: MP Eva statue at the end of Death
wikitext:
[]: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by geistygal » Thu Sep 30, 2021 9:07 pm Hi! So I watched Death for the first time tonight (not Death(true) or (true)^2), and noticed that the concrete(?) structure that Kaworu was sitting on when he met Shinji looks almost identical to a decapitated Mass Produced Evangelion. How is this possible? Is this just Anno teasing the content that was to come, or is it actually supposed to be there?

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Sep 30, 2021 9:24 pm Ah yes, there's been a lot of old discussion about that bizarre statue. It seems to be a mashup between the traditional depiction of an Angel, a petrified headless Mass Production Eva, and Lilith all at once. thread/3736/whats-that-statue/?hilit=statue thread/4447/Angel-Statue-from-Episode-24/?hilit=statue One of the best analyses ever of the statue, its relationship to Kaworu and its multiple layers of symbolism was made by Reichu on the ancient Eva Commentary site, link below - https://web.archive.org/web/20071107153 ... hizou.html

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by RussianRiz » Thu Sep 30, 2021 10:01 pm I just love the symbolism present in the statue. It itself makes no sense in being something we've seen before, but can be a tease of the future content, like the Evas-MP, as like you said. It has the clear resemblance that Ursus cited above. The parallel with Kaworu is very apparent. It sounds like a headless fallen angel, just as Kaworu ends his appearance.

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Fri Oct 01, 2021 10:17 am I've always imagined this statue to be the remains of Unit 00. It exploded alongside the 16th angel and that's what's left of it. Makes sense, right? It's in the exact place where Ayanami died and looks like an Evangelion. We all know Unit 00 was gone by episode 24, afterall. I obviously know it's too small to be Unit 00. However, I think it's definitely connected to what happened in episode 23. Maybe that's what happened to the Evangelion's core...

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Oct 01, 2021 10:59 am @Bernardo - interesting that you should make that suggestion... UrsusArctos on 19 October 2007 wrote: We never ever see a statue like this anywhere else before in the series, and it only comes up along with Kaworu in the very end. Combine that with the bleeding, and I wonder if that's Kentucky Fried Eva-00's remains, compressed in size. Apart from the obvious Lilith/MPE resemblances outlined in the article, there really is no data on that Statue's origin. Tokyo-3 is an odd place to keep a wierd Angel statue like that, where'd it come?

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Oct 04, 2021 10:10 am I would like to point out that this statue appears to have breasts, as does Unit 00 after merging with the 16th angel. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by EvaChero » Mon Oct 04, 2021 12:12 pm I'm so glad this was pointed out as I missed it completely, but it just makes sense to that it's the remains Unit 0 ...it really fits other wise what other reason would a statue with these characteristics be there for? Maybe the scale is off a bit but it wouldn't be the first time right? I guess tho' explaining it "in universe" one would have to figure out where all the water came from?? Or were they turning every combat crater into a pond/lake?

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by geistygal » Wed Oct 06, 2021 9:14 am Wow, just wanna say, I love this community already. Was worried (and almost certain) that this had been discussed before, and I'm thrilled that we can still discuss this in length despite being an older topic. It never occurred to me that it could be the remains of Unit 00! I like that theory the most :3

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by AWinters » Tue Mar 01, 2022 12:18 pm I've been mulling this over for some time and now I believe that the answer to this is referenced in the Manga. (Hidden for those who haven't read the Manga yet) SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Mar 01, 2022 3:16 pm For that matter, 3.0+1.0 has much the same take on things .

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by AWinters » Tue Mar 01, 2022 3:57 pm Mr. Tines wrote: For that matter, 3.0+1.0 has much the same take on things .

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Mar 19, 2022 3:54 pm Wow, I had never considered the Unit-00 connection, and it clicks well in a way that seem obvious in retrospect. The only major issue, as brought up, is that the statue is clearly much smaller than an Eva. But let's compare that to what happened with Adam, who famously made becoming a being of light and then shrinking in size cool during Second Impact. Albeit, Unit-00 in this comparison becomes petrified, while Adam becomes a feasible embryo for.. reasons, but this can probably be handwaved as Unit-00 being an imperfect copy of Adam, and would also neatly foreshadow the Mass Production Evas becoming petrified in EoE. I like this theory, and am now incorporating it into my head canon. Thanks, Bernardo!

[]: Re: MP-Eva statue at the end of Death?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Mar 19, 2022 7:35 pm @Sachi: I had come up with the same thought all the way back in 2007 ! AWinters wrote: For me, this helps confirm that a continued chain of timelines was always the plan, even during the making of the original series. Now I'm able to rewatch the series with a new perspective.
